headline: Bank of America's Board of Directors approved a $41 million 2025 total compensation package for CEO Brian Moynihan, while raising the performance standards required for future equity payouts.
investor_impact: The revised compensation structure strongly aligns CEO incentives with aggressive long-term growth, requiring unprecedented net income levels of up to $37 billion annually to achieve maximum equity payouts. This signals strong Board confidence in the bank's future profitability and operational leverage.
key_figures: $92 billion (aggregate net income needed from 2026-2028 for 100% target payout)
$112 billion (aggregate net income needed from 2026-2028 for 150% maximum payout)
10.5% (Target 3-year average growth of adjusted tangible book value)
